data.title: Viktor Orbán's Defeat: Implications for Hungary, EU, and Global Politics
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting a discussion on the Big Take podcast where @AmbPressman reflects on the recent political shift in Hungary. Viktor Orbán, who has maintained a stronghold on Hungarian politics for decades, faced a surprising defeat. This development is significant not only for Hungary but also for the European Union, Russia, and the global political landscape, including the MAGA movement. The podcast delves into the potential ramifications of this change in leadership and its broader impact on international relations and political dynamics.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Progress in Negotiations Amid Potential Talks
data.description:
The United States and Iran are reportedly making progress in their ongoing negotiations, according to a U.S. official cited by CBS and NBC. According to Odaily, discussions are underway within the U.S. government regarding the details of a potential second face-to-face meeting before the expiration of a temporary ceasefire agreement between the two nations. The feasibility of this meeting depends on the progress of communications in the coming days, with officials considering possible dates and locations.

A regional source revealed that before Islamabad, Pakistan, was chosen as the previous negotiation site, other locations such as Geneva, Switzerland, Vienna, Austria, and Istanbul, Turkey, were considered. Currently, Geneva and Islamabad are again under consideration. Depending on the progress of discussions in the coming days, the U.S. and Iran might extend the ceasefire by two weeks to allow more time for negotiations.

Additionally, according to a report by RIA Novosti citing a journalist from The Atlantic on social media, the next round of direct talks between the U.S. and Iran could take place on the 16th in Islamabad.

data.title: US media: US-Iran contacts continue, and further face-to-face talks may be held.
data.description: Both CBS and NBC News, citing a US official, reported that contacts between the US and Iran are continuing and that "progress is being made in reaching an agreement." CNN, citing sources, reported that US government officials are internally discussing details of a second face-to-face meeting before the expiration of the temporary ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran. Whether the talks can take place depends on progress in exchanges over the next few days, and officials are discussing potential dates and locations. The report also stated that a "regional source" revealed that several alternative locations were proposed before Islamabad, Pakistan, was finalized as the location for the last round of negotiations, including Geneva, Switzerland; Vienna, Austria; and Istanbul, Turkey. Currently, Geneva and Islamabad are again under consideration. According to sources, depending on the progress of exchanges over the next few days, the US and Iran may extend the ceasefire for another two weeks to allow more time for negotiations. Furthermore, RIA Novosti, citing a social media post by a reporter from The Atlantic, reported on the 13th that the next round of "direct negotiations" between the US and Iran may be held in Islamabad on the 16th.

data.title: White House advisors say differences in encryption legislation are converging and progress is accelerating.
data.description: White House digital asset advisor Patrick Witt stated that previously considered "intractable" disagreements regarding crypto legislation have significantly decreased, and the prospects for passing related legislation are improving. With Congress reconvening, a key Senate committee is expected to vote on a comprehensive crypto bill, paving the way for further legislation. This bill aims to clarify the regulatory division of labor between the SEC and CFTC and establish a unified regulatory framework for exchanges and market participants. Current controversy remains focused on stablecoin incentive mechanisms. While the passed stablecoin bill prohibits issuers from directly paying interest to users, disagreements persist regarding whether third-party platforms should be allowed to provide rewards. Overall, policy-making is shifting from "high uncertainty" to "gradual convergence," and the regulatory framework for the crypto industry is expected to enter a phase of substantial progress.

data.title: Former CFTC Chairman Giancarlo has transitioned to a full-time career as a crypto and technology advisor.
data.description: Former U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Chairman Chris Giancarlo has announced his departure from the legal profession to become a full-time advisor in the crypto and technology sector. Giancarlo served as chairman of the CFTC from 2017 to 2019 and was known as the "Crypto Dad" for his support of the crypto industry. During his tenure, he spearheaded the launch of the world's first regulated Bitcoin futures and established the LabCFTC innovation division. After leaving office, he has advised numerous organizations, including prediction market platforms and stablecoin companies, and co-founded the Digital Dollar Project to advance research into central bank digital currencies. Going forward, Giancarlo will focus on providing strategic consulting to entrepreneurs and companies in the fintech and digital asset sectors, and will continue to be involved in public policy research and non-profit projects.

data.title: BlackRock reverts to an overweight rating on US equities, believing the impact of the Iran war is manageable.
data.description: BlackRock strategists have reinstated their overweight view on U.S. equities, believing the impact of the Middle East conflict on global economic growth "may be contained." After downgrading their risk appetite to neutral several weeks ago due to escalating Middle East tensions, a team of strategists led by Jean Boivin, head of BlackRock's Investment Institute, said in a report on Monday that they have been watching "two signals that increase risk exposure," including the resumption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and signs that the war's economic impact is limited. They stated, "We have seen progress in both areas," adding that a near-term ceasefire is "crucial" and the threshold for a return to war is "high." BlackRock also highlighted the upcoming earnings season. "Even during the conflict, corporate earnings expectations have continued to rise, partly thanks to the artificial intelligence theme." Regarding U.S. equities, BlackRock stated, "The manageable impact of the Middle East conflict on global growth, coupled with strong earnings expectations—particularly in the technology sector—maintains our risk appetite."

data.title: Former CFTC Chair Chris Giancarlo Transitions to Corporate Advisory Role in Crypto and Tech
data.description: Former CFTC Chair Chris Giancarlo has announced his retirement from law to focus on his new role as a corporate advisor in the crypto and tech sectors. According to NS3.AI, Giancarlo shared on X that he will be advising founders, CEOs, and boards in fintech and digital assets. He plans to continue his involvement in public policy research and nonprofit activities, leveraging his extensive experience in the industry.

data.title: FBI Detains Suspect in Connection with Attack on OpenAI CEO's Home
data.description: The FBI, in collaboration with its partners, conducted an operation in Texas this morning, resulting in the detention of a suspect linked to last Friday's attack on the home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. According to Jin10, the operation marks a significant development in the investigation into the incident, which had raised concerns about the safety of prominent figures in the tech industry. The FBI has not released further details about the suspect or the motive behind the attack. The investigation is ongoing as authorities work to ensure the security of tech leaders and their families.

data.title: Oracle Shares Surge 11% Following AI Enhancements Announcement
data.description: Oracle's stock experienced an 11% increase on Monday after the company revealed new AI enhancements for utilities. According to NS3.AI, Wall Street analysts maintain a Strong Buy consensus on Oracle, with an average price target of $245.11, suggesting a potential upside of 69.98% for ORCL.

data.title: Tulsi Gabbard Declassifies Transcripts Related to Trump's First Impeachment
data.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has declassified transcripts from closed-door sessions held in 2019 concerning U.S. President Donald Trump's first impeachment. According to NS3.AI, these records detail briefings with Michael Atkinson, the then-Intelligence Community Inspector General, regarding a whistleblower complaint. The transcripts include allegations of undisclosed partisan affiliations and previous interactions with staff from the Schiff committee.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Close Higher as Nasdaq Leads Gains
data.description: U.S. stocks ended the trading session on a positive note, with the Nasdaq Composite leading the gains by rising 1.23%. According to NS3.AI, the S&P 500 also saw an increase of 1.02%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.63%. These gains reflect a positive sentiment in the market, contributing to an overall upward trend in major indices.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts 59% Chance of Trump Signing Crypto Bill This Year
data.description: Polymarket has estimated a 59% probability that U.S. President Donald Trump will sign a crypto-related bill this year. According to NS3.AI, this prediction pertains to legislation associated with the Crypto Market Structure Act, commonly referred to as CLARITY.

data.title: USDT and USDC Active Addresses on Ethereum Reach 2026 Low Amid Market Stagnation
data.description: Active addresses for USDT and USDC on the Ethereum network have declined to their lowest level in 2026, according to Santiment. This decrease is commonly observed during extended periods of market stagnation, where prices remain within a narrow range. According to NS3.AI, such trends often indicate a lack of significant market movement or investor activity.

data.title: BlackRock Strategists Resume Overweight Stance on U.S. Stocks Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
BlackRock strategists have reinstated their overweight position on U.S. stocks, citing the manageable impact of the Middle East conflict on global economic growth. According to Jin10, the team, led by Jean Boivin from the BlackRock Investment Institute, had previously adopted a neutral stance due to escalating tensions in the region. In a report released on Monday, they noted that they have been monitoring two key signals indicating increased risk exposure: the resumption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and signs that the war's economic impact is limited. They stated, "We have seen progress in both areas," emphasizing the importance of the recent ceasefire and the high threshold for returning to conflict.

BlackRock also highlighted the upcoming earnings season, noting that corporate profit expectations have risen even during the conflict, partly driven by AI themes. Regarding U.S. stocks, BlackRock remarked, "The impact of the Middle East conflict on global growth is controllable, coupled with strong earnings expectations—especially in the tech sector—allowing us to maintain our risk appetite."

data.title: Zambia Suspends Fuel Taxes Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: Zambia has announced a three-month suspension of fuel taxes, a move expected to result in a revenue loss of approximately $100 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's decision aimed at protecting consumers from escalating oil prices, which have been influenced by the ongoing conflict in Iran. The suspension is part of a broader strategy to mitigate the economic impact on citizens as global oil markets react to geopolitical tensions. Zambia's government is closely monitoring the situation and exploring additional measures to stabilize the domestic economy.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 73,000 USDT with a 2.62% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 13, 2026, 19:32 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 73,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 73,017.703125 USDT, with a narrowed 2.62% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: IMF Chief Urges Nations to Avoid Harmful Energy Restrictions Amid Global Crises
data.description: International Monetary Fund (IMF) Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has called on countries to 'avoid causing harm' by refraining from implementing energy restrictions that could exacerbate existing crises. According to Jin10, Georgieva highlighted the impact of war-related crises on regions such as Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, and South Pacific island nations. She warned that a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could have severe consequences for economic growth and inflation.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Discusses AI Model Mythos with U.S. Government Amid Pentagon Dispute
data.description:
On April 14, Jin10 reported that Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark stated on Monday that the company is in discussions with the Trump administration regarding its advanced AI model, Mythos. This comes despite the Pentagon halting business with Anthropic following a contract dispute. The disagreement between Anthropic and the Pentagon centers on security constraints related to the military's use of Anthropic's AI tools, leading the Pentagon last month to classify Anthropic as a supply chain risk, thereby prohibiting its contractors from using Anthropic's products.

"We have a narrow contract dispute, but I don't want this to affect our strong focus on national security," said Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark in Washington. "Our position is that the government must understand these technologies... so we are certainly discussing Mythos with them and will communicate about subsequent models."

The nature and details of the negotiations between Anthropic and the U.S. government, including which agencies are involved, remain unclear.

data.title: Exodus Files Lawsuit to Enforce $175 Million Acquisition of W3C
data.description: Exodus has initiated legal proceedings against W3C and its CEO, Garth Howat, in Delaware Chancery Court, seeking to compel the completion of a $175 million acquisition deal agreed upon in November of the previous year. According to NS3.AI, the lawsuit claims that Exodus had already extended $80 million in loans at the time of signing, which included a personal loan of $10 million to Howat. The legal action alleges that W3C and Howat have taken measures to avoid finalizing the acquisition.

data.title: Digital Asset Market Clarity Act Progresses in U.S. Senate
data.description: Negotiators are reportedly overcoming the final obstacles to advance the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act in the U.S. Senate. According to NS3.AI, Patrick Witt stated that the stablecoin yield compromise remains in place. However, the bill still requires a markup hearing by the Senate Banking Committee before it can proceed to a final vote in the Senate.
